execution_overview.overall_summary: Built a Python 3.11 Docker image and executed the planned Spearman correlation analysis between MiniK_Total and DSM5_Total using a dependency-free Python script due to import errors with numpy/scipy. The result shows a strong negative association (rho ≈ -0.590, one-sided p ≈ 0). This supports the focal claim for both Task1 and Task2.

deviations.description: Rewrote the analysis script to use only the Python standard library (implemented Spearman correlation via rank transformation and Pearson correlation; computed p-values via large-sample normal approximation) due to missing numpy/scipy in the container at runtime.
deviations.reason: The original Python translation imported numpy/pandas/scipy and failed with ModuleNotFoundError: numpy.

method_fidelity.fidelity_note: Followed the planned Spearman correlation analysis and one-sided inference; only the implementation changed to avoid missing dependencies, with approximate p-values.

other.p_value_note: Large-sample normal approximation for Spearman's rho.

result_raw.result_text: MiniK_Total and DSM5_Total are strongly negatively correlated (rho ≈ -0.590), with one-sided p ≈ 0 (approximation).

conclusion.conclusion_text: The result supports the focal claim: faster life strategy (lower MiniK_Total) is associated with greater psychopathology (higher DSM5_Total), yielding a negative correlation.

deviations.description: Standard-library rewrite of the Spearman correlation to avoid external dependencies; approximate p-values used.
deviations.reason: Container lacked numpy/scipy at runtime causing import failure.

method_fidelity.fidelity_note: Same analysis path as Task1; Spearman correlation with one-sided 'less' test using Mini-K as the predictor.

other.p_value_note: Large-sample normal approximation for Spearman's rho.

result_raw.result_text: MiniK_Total and DSM5_Total are strongly negatively correlated (rho ≈ -0.590), with one-sided p ≈ 0 (approximation).

conclusion.conclusion_text: Using Mini-K as the life history measure, results support the focal claim with a strong negative correlation.
